LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANGIE AQUINO'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. --- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 128.7(C)(1). DEFENDANTS, THEREFORE, WERE NOT ACCORDED THEIR FULL 21 DAYS TO WITHDRAW THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO OBTAIN AN ORDER SHORTENING THE 21 DAY PERIOD UNDER CCP 128.7(C)(1) OR TO ASK THAT THE MOTION TO VACATE BE CONTINUED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF ANGIE AQUINO'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Allan Schwartz, Esq. of Law Offices of Allan Schwartz 956-0712 for pltf & moving party; Steven S. Miyake, Esq. 421-4400 for defts & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 128.7(c)(1). DEFENDANTS, THEREFORE, WERE NOT ACCORDED THEIR FULL 21 DAYS TO WITHDRAW THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO OBTAIN AN ORDER SHORTENING THE 21 DAY PERIOD UNDER CCP 128.7(c)(1) OR TO ASK THAT THE MOTION TO VACATE BE CONTINUED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice And Motion For Order Compelling Responses Of Deft Real Estate Unlimited To Request For Production Of Documents And Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Allan Schwartz, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: ANGIE AQUINO

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
PLAINTIFF ANGIE AQUINO's Motion To Compel Defendant Real Estate Unlimited To Respond To Request For Production Of Documents is granted. Defendant Real Estate Unlimited's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant Real Estate Unlimited is ordered to respond within 20 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant Real Estate Unlimited in the amount of $600, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.
